files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 8de79bd..90f266f 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -89,2 +89,9 @@ Day 84 / Week 12 observations:
 - Support spend trim did not create an aged backlog in the database: open issues are still mostly same-day and resolved issues averaged 1 day, though social posts are still critical of specific blocking bugs/SLA transparency.
 - Week 12 adjustment: revert B/C prices to restore conversion, keep S2 content funded, keep total ops spend flat but shift S1-targeted support into S2, and post a specific blocking-bug SLA message rather than aggregate metrics.
+
+Day 91 / Week 13 observations:
+- Reverting B/C worked: cash rose to $179k as subscription payments reached $186k against roughly $148k in weekly costs.
+- S2 content generated 362 paid subscribers from 2,503 leads, with C recovering strongly to 248 subscribers at $179. B softened to 97, but the overall paid funnel is profitable again.
+- Usage peaked just over 1.0M/day, so tier 3 capacity is still required; tier 2 would overload.
+- Open issues are rising but still mostly same-day. Social criticism now centers on SLA enforcement, support handoffs, and usage limits rather than outages.
+- Week 13 adjustment: cautiously scale S2 content spend, add a little S2 support capacity, keep dev steady, and raise B/C quotas to improve professional value without making A too generous.
